1.label: Level 1
1.title: Operations Recovery
1.headline: A compact network breaks under fog, runway loss, and aircraft faults.
incidents: Dense fog slows Delhi departures and breaks the morning bank.
Mumbai loses one runway just as west-coast departures peak.
An IndiGo aircraft reports hydraulics and blocks a ready crew.

2.label: Level 2
2.title: Passenger-Aware Network
2.headline: Connections, emergency priority, and gate failures turn delay into passenger harm.
incidents: Delhi spacing delays now threaten Mumbai and Bengaluru connections.
Hyderabad needs emergency arrival priority inside a packed wave.
Kolkata passengers are close to overnight stranding.

3.label: Level 3
3.title: Economic Multi-Agent Control
3.headline: Airlines negotiate for scarce slots while the tower must stay neutral.
incidents: Mumbai fuel trucks are short-staffed; every hold burns cash.
IndiGo, Air India, Akasa Air, and SpiceJet demand Bengaluru evening slots.
Goa weather pushes low-fuel aircraft toward alternate airports.

4.label: Level 4
4.title: IndiGo Crisis Replay
4.headline: A December 2025-style crew availability crisis cancels hundreds of flights.
incidents: FDTL-style crew constraints leave many aircraft without legal crews.
Mumbai passenger queues overflow after repeated IndiGo cancellations.
Bengaluru must rebuild rotations without breaking next-day operations.
